text: The GOG system shall simulate catalog data, auxiliary data, and intermediate main database data for Gaia instruments.
The GOG system shall use error models to provide simulations of mission data.
The GOG system shall utilize the GaiaSimu library for Universe and spacecraft models.
The GOG system shall provide an option to add calibration errors to simulated data.
The GOG system shall output simulated data according to the Gaia Main Database Interface Control Document.
The GOG system shall remain modular to accommodate continuous evolution of error models.
The GOG system shall be configurable through an input configuration file.
The GOG system shall generate source data from the GaiaSimu library Universe model.
The GOG system shall generate source data from a user-provided input object catalog.
The GOG system shall process astronomical sources on a source-by-source basis.
The GOG system shall adjust error models based on local source density if specified by the user.
The GOG system shall produce Universe Model catalog data as a system output.
The GOG system shall produce main database epoch data for each transit of a source.
The GOG system shall produce main database combined data for all source transits.
The GOG system shall calculate end-of-mission data based on a user-specified number of transits.
The GOG system shall provide user-selectable noise generation effects.
The GOG system shall simulate instrumental noise from the observation process.
The GOG system shall simulate reduction noise added during scientific content extraction.
The GOG system shall provide identical results for identical input configuration parameters.
The GOG system shall be executable from a command line.
The GOG system should operate within cluster or grid environments.
The GOG system shall be built as a standalone Java application.
The GOG system shall be deployable as a JAR file.
The GOG system shall execute in single thread mode on grid environments.
The GOG system shall support execution on Linux, Unix, and Mac computers.
The GOG system shall generate data for at least 20 percent of the expected final Gaia catalogue.
The GOG system shall process one complete source in a maximum of 2 CPU seconds.
The Initialisation Module shall load and store the simulation configuration.
The Initialisation Module shall provide a common interface to access configuration values.
The Initialisation Module should read input source information from the Universe Model.
The Initialisation Module should read input source information from user-provided sources.
The Initialisation Module must include the Galaxy model within the universe model.
The Initialisation Module must include the SkyBackground model within the universe model.
The Initialisation Module must include the Extinction Law model within the universe model.
The Initialisation Module must include the selected scanning law within the instrument model.
The Initialisation Module must include instrument dead time within the instrument model.
The Initialisation Module must include noise models within the instrument model.
The Initialisation Module must include instrument effect subtraction within the reduction model.
The Initialisation Module must include scientific content extraction noise within the reduction model.
The Initialisation Module shall define the simulation output parameters for spectrum and astrometry.
The Initialisation Module shall define the simulation output parameters for physical parameters and flags.
The Epoch Parameters Module shall determine transit times for epoch data.
The Epoch Parameters Module shall determine field angles in each CCD for epoch data.
The Epoch Parameters Module shall calculate mean transit times for each source transit.
The Epoch Parameters Module shall account for gate activation during astrometric parameter calculation.
The Epoch Parameters Module shall account for signal loss due to window cutoff.
The Epoch Parameters Module shall determine epoch photometry including G, GRP, GBP, and GRVS magnitudes.
The Epoch Parameters Module shall determine the epoch radial velocity including instrumental and reduction errors.
The Epoch Parameters Module shall determine epoch flags.
The Epoch Parameters Module shall determine epoch physical parameters including effective temperature and surface gravity.
The Combined Parameters Module shall compute astrometric combined data using all previous transits.
The Combined Parameters Module shall compute parallax and proper motion errors.
The Combined Parameters Module shall determine the covariance matrix for combined astrometry.
The Combined Parameters Module shall determine estimated errors for astrometric parameters at the mean epoch.
The Combined Parameters Module shall determine the combined data photometry including mean G and GRP.
The Combined Parameters Module shall determine the mean radial velocity.
The Combined Parameters Module shall determine the final mission flags for variability and non-single stars.
The Combined Parameters Module shall determine mean physical parameters including iron abundance and iron-to-hydrogen ratio.
The Spectrum Simulation Module shall simulate low-resolution and high-resolution spectra.
The Spectrum Simulation Module shall add flux noise including Poisson and detection noise to simulated spectra.
The Spectrum Simulation Module shall add CCD combination noise for RVS spectra.
The Spectrum Simulation Module shall add wavelength calibration noise to simulated spectra.
The Spectrum Simulation Module should add multi-transit combination noise for combined spectral data.
The Output Module shall write universe model catalog data in accordance with the Universe Model ICD.
The Output Module should use the GaiaSimu library to write output formats.
The Output Module shall follow the MDB data model for simulation output.
The Output Module shall distribute data extracts to the Data Processing Centers.
